subtasks.description: Query Postgres to retrieve all licenses expiring within the next 6 months, including license ID, product name, vendor, expiration date, and current status
Reconcile the expiring licenses data with historical usage patterns, contract terms, and cost trends to generate renewal recommendations (renew, renegotiate, replace, or discontinue) for each license

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks required: (1) sql_gen (small tier) for straightforward SELECT query on Postgres with date-range filtering; (2) cross_recon (mid tier) to synthesize the license data with business context (usage, cost, alternatives) and generate recommendations. Cross_recon handles the recommendation logic rather than a separate policy_action because renewal prioritization is reconciliation-driven analysis, not policy compliance checking. Mid tier sufficient for cross_recon because recommendations are based on standard business signals without complex multi-source signal fusion.

output.sql: SELECT DISTINCT cf.license_id, cf.product, invoice.vendor_name, invoice.due_date AS expiration_date, invoice.status FROM consumption_facts cf JOIN invoice_facts invoice ON cf.license_id = invoice.contract_id WHERE invoice.due_date >= CURRENT_DATE AND invoice.due_date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '6 months' ORDER BY invoice.due_date ASC
